dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7417 IH: Women’s Heart Health Expansion Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mrs. Beatty (for herself, Mr. Smith of New Jersey, Ms. DeLauro, Mr. Ciscomani, Mr. Fitzpatrick, Mr. Bishop, Mr. Lawler, Ms. Lois Frankel of Florida, Ms. Moore of Wisconsin, Mrs. Dingell, Mrs. Cherfilus-McCormick, and Ms. Brown) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce

form.official-title: To amend the Public Health Service Act to reauthorize the WISEWOMAN program.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Women’s Heart Health Expansion Act of 2026.
Section 1509 of the Public Health Service Act (42 U.S.C. 300n–4a) is amended to read as follows:

section.header: Supplemental grants for additional preventive health services

subsection.text: The Secretary, acting through the Director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, may award supplemental grants to recipients of grants under section 1501 to carry out projects for the purposes of—

paragraph.text: providing preventive health services in addition to the services authorized in such section, including—

subparagraph.text: screenings regarding blood pressure and cholesterol; and

paragraph.text: providing appropriate referrals for medical treatment of women receiving services pursuant to paragraph (1) and ensuring, to the extent practicable, the provision of appropriate follow-up services; and
evaluating activities conducted under paragraphs (1) and (2) through appropriate surveillance or program-monitoring activities.

subsection.text: Women eligible for services pursuant to a grant under subsection (a) shall include—

paragraph.text: women receiving services pursuant to a grant made under section 1501; and
other women who are low-income and meet such eligibility criteria as the Secretary may specify.

subsection.text: Services provided pursuant to a grant under subsection (a) shall be provided by—

paragraph.text: an entity screening women for breast and cervical cancer pursuant to a grant under section 1501; or
other entities that the grant recipient may designate, subject to the approval of such designation by the Secretary.

subsection.text: To carry out this section, there is authorized to be appropriated $250,000,000 for the period of fiscal years 2027 through 2031.
